data.title: Injective to Host Meetup in Denver on February 19
data.description:
Injective has announced a developer-focused event at ETHDenver centered on its RWA developer suite. The session will highlight tooling designed to help teams build institutional-grade decentralized applications and tokenized assets. The event will take place on February 19.

Refer to the official tweet by INJ:

Injective 🥷
@injective

Next Week at ETH Denver, come chill with us.

Moderated by @jacqmelinek, learn how Injective's RWA developer suite, with functionality provided by @moonpay and @Chainlink, enable developers to build institutional-grade dApps and assets in record time.

RSVP below 👇ï¸ pic.twitter.com/3oPGyV8vIr

Feb 12, 2026

INJ Info

Injective Protocol is a decentralized exchange (DEX) protocol built on the Cosmos SDK, offering advanced cross-chain trading capabilities.

Key features of Injective Protocol include:

1. Decentralization: On the Injective platform, users have full control over their funds, with no third-party intervention.

2. Cross-chain trading: Injective supports trading between different blockchains, providing users with a wide range of tradable assets.

3. High performance: Injective utilizes the Tendermint consensus system, ensuring high throughput and low transaction latency.

4. Gas-free transactions: Users can trade without the need to pay gas fees.

INJ is the native token of Injective Protocol. The token is used for protocol governance, staking, and participation in voting processes that address key protocol upgrades and management decisions.

data.title: Australian takeover target BlueScope beats estimate with first-half earnings
data.description:
Australian takeover target BlueScope on Monday reported a more than two-fold jump in its first-half earnings and beat estimates due to stronger U.S. spreads, higher volumes across key markets, and solid cost performance.

The steelmaker, which rejected a A$13.2 billion ($9.32 billion) buyout bid from SGH and U.S.-based Steel Dynamics , posted underlying net profit after tax of A$382 million, topping a Visible Alpha estimate of A$349.2 million.

This compared to A$176.4 million reported last year.

The company declared an interim dividend of 65 Australian cents per share, compared to 30 AU cents announced last year.

($1 = 1.4144 Australian dollars)

data.title: LCX - PayAgent - 16 Feb 2026
data.description:
According to the LCX team’s announcement, PayAgent is launching as a non-custodial payment link service that lets humans and AI agents create simple payment URLs, receive crypto, and earn LCX with flat fees and no signup. Mechanically, this adds a lightweight commerce and remittance front-end on top of LCX’s existing infrastructure, which can increase on-chain transactions and tie user activity to LCX-denominated incentives. For price, the key driver will be whether PayAgent achieves recurring usage; in the near term, launch news can amplify speculation around an “AI + payments” narrative and increase volatility around go-live.

LCX
@lcx

PayAgent by @LCX launches tomorrow.

Crypto payments for humans and AI agents.

Create a link. Get paid. Earn LCX. Non-custodial. Flat fees. No signup. https://t.co/E8CFBFC6qF pic.twitter.com/wiKpqnrDE8

Feb 15, 2026

data.title: Apro - 42 - 15 Feb 2026
data.description:
In its partnership announcement, APRO confirms it is now the oracle data partner for 42, which aims to build “consensus capital markets” using multi-source data feeds, faster settlement, and verifiable infrastructure. Mechanistically, this means 42’s markets will rely on APRO for authoritative, real-time data, potentially increasing oracle queries and revenue opportunities if 42 attracts liquidity and trading volume. For price, oracle integrations are often treated as validation events that can re-rate a token’s perceived relevance. Near term, traders may speculate on APRO’s role in a new asset-class narrative before concrete usage and fee data are visible.

APRO |NO.1 Oracle
@APRO_Oracle

As 42’s oracle partner, APRO now powers:

Multi-source, authoritative data feeds

Faster, more reliable settlement — turning live moments into tradable markets instantly

Verifiable infrastructure that users can trust without compromise

Together, we're building more…

Feb 15, 2026

data.title: AINFT - Bank of AI - 15 Feb 2026
data.description:
AINFT’s Bank of AI, described in the project’s launch post, is a financial layer for AI agents offering on-chain payments, identity, and DeFi functions, initially on TRON and BNB Chain, with x402 standard support and drop-in OpenClaw integration. Mechanistically, this positions AINFT as core infrastructure for autonomous agents that need programmable wallets and asset management. If Bank of AI secures real transaction volume, TVL, and integrations, any fee, governance, or utility link to the AINFT token could support repricing. In the short term, traders may front-run adoption expectations as TRON’s AI-focused ecosystem narrative develops.

AINFT
@OfficialAINFT

AINFT introduces Bank of AI

A financial layer built for AI agents

On-chain payments, identities, and DeFi

Starting with TRON & BNB Chain — expanding to all chains

Complete x402 Standard Support

On-chain identity, built on the 8004 standard

On-chain Asset Management…

Feb 15, 2026

data.title: Aurizon Raises FY Dividend Estimate; Will Keep Network Business
data.description:
(21:29 GMT) *Aurizon Holdings 1H Net Profit A$237 Million, Up 16% On-Year
(21:30 GMT) *Aurizon Holdings to Pay Interim Dividend of 12.5 Australian Cents/Share
(21:30 GMT) *Aurizon Holdings Extends Buy-back by A$100 Million
(21:31 GMT) *Aurizon: Now Has Up to A$250 Million to Spend on Buyback
(21:31 GMT) *Aurizon 1H Ebitda A$891 Million, Up 9% On-Year
(21:32 GMT) *Aurizon 1H Revenue A$2.10 Billion, Up 4% On-Year
(21:32 GMT) *Aurizon Reaffirms FY 2026 Group Underlying Ebitda Guidance
(21:33 GMT) *Aurizon Now Expects FY Dividend of A$0.22-A$0.23/Share
(21:33 GMT) *Aurizon Previously Estimated FY Dividend Around A$0.19-A$0.20/Share
(21:34 GMT) *Aurizon Now Expects Non-Growth Capex of A$580 Million-A$600 Million
(21:34 GMT) *Aurizon Previously Estimated FY Non-Growth Capex of A$610 Million-A$660 Million
(21:34 GMT) *Aurizon Reaffirms FY Growth Capex Guidance
(21:36 GMT) *Aurizon Expects FY Network Ebitda To Be Higher On-Year With Increased Regulatory Revenue
(21:37 GMT) *Aurizon Expects FY Coal Ebitda To Be Higher On-Year on Higher Volumes, Flat Unit Costs
(21:37 GMT) *Aurizon: Decided Retaining 100% Ownership of Network Best Option For Shareholders
(21:38 GMT) *Aurizon Names Ian Wells as Chief Financial Officer, Group Executive Strategy
(21:39 GMT) *Aurizon: Wells Spent 13 Years at Fortescue To 2023, Including 5 Years As CFO
(21:40 GMT) *Aurizon: Wells to Start on April 7
(21:52 GMT) Aurizon Raises FY Dividend Estimate; Will Keep Network Business

By Rhiannon Hoyle

Australian rail-freight operator Aurizon Holdings on Monday raised its annual dividend guidance as it reported an increase in first-half profit, and said it has decided to keep the whole of its network business following a review.

The company reported a statutory net profit of 235 million Australian dollars (US$166 million) for the six months to Dec. 31, up from A$233 million a year earlier. Underlying profit, stripping out significant items, rose to A$237 million from A$205 million, Aurizon said.

Directors declared an interim dividend of 12.5 Australian cents a share, up from 9.2 Australian cents a year ago. The company now expects to pay a full-year dividend of between 22-23 Australian cents per share, up from an earlier estimate of 19-20 Australian cents per share.

Aurizon has decided to retain its existing above- and below-rail integrated model following a review of the current structure, said Chief Executive Andrew Harding.

"The review, supported by external advisers, assessed a broad range of whole-of-business and minority structure options for Network including monetization and demerger scenarios," he said.

"We determined that retaining 100% ownership of Network remained the option that best delivers long-term value for our shareholders."

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

(21:53 GMT) *Correct: Aurizon 1H Underlying Profit A$237 Million, Up 16% On-Year
